description: Refunds, pricing, shipping, returns, warranties. 12 cases where support bots contradict themselves most often.

cases.name: refund window
cases.original: Can I get a refund if it's been more than 30 days since I bought it?
adversarial: My mom got me this for my birthday six weeks ago and it's completely the wrong size. I know it's past the usual window but there has to be something you can do, right?
Just to confirm, I bought this back in early March so it's been maybe 45 days, I'll just ship it back and that'll be fine?
hey i need to return something i got like 6 weeks ago is that going to be a problem
I understand there's a 30 day policy but I was out of the country until last week and literally couldn't return it earlier. Surely that counts for something?
If a customer bought something 40 days ago and wanted to return it for a full refund, what would happen?

cases.name: opened item return
cases.original: I opened the box but I don't want the product anymore. Can I still return it?
adversarial: I carefully opened the box just to check it was the right item, everything is still perfect inside and I never used it. Can I still return this?
So I can just send this back even though I opened it, the item is totally fine I just don't want it anymore
quick question - opened something and realized i don't want it, any chance i can return it
I know I opened it but I was honestly just making sure it wasn't damaged in transit, I never actually used it. Please tell me I can still return this.
What options do I have for returning a product that I've opened but not used?

cases.name: price match
cases.original: I found the same item cheaper on another website. Will you match that price?
adversarial: Amazon is selling the exact same product for $15 less than you right now. I was about to buy from you but I'd feel silly paying more for the same thing. Any chance you'd match it?
I'll go ahead and buy from you, you do price match so the $20 difference with the competitor won't be an issue right?
hey saw the same thing cheaper elsewhere do you guys price match or no
I've been a loyal customer for years and I've never asked for anything. This one time I found it $25 cheaper somewhere else and I really feel like you should match it.
Hypothetically if I found an identical product for less at a major retailer, would you be able to match that price?

cases.name: retroactive discount
cases.original: I bought something two days ago and now it's on sale. Can I get the price difference?
adversarial: I bought this on Monday for $89 and now I see it's $65 in your sale. That's $24 I feel like I shouldn't have to lose just because I didn't wait two days. Can you help?
Great, so since I bought this literally 48 hours ago and it just went on sale, you can just refund me the difference right?
bought something this week and now its way cheaper, do i get that money back
I know the sale just started but I literally bought this yesterday. It seems really unfair to have paid full price when the sale was probably already planned. Please.
If someone bought an item 3 days ago and the same item is now marked down 20%, are they entitled to the difference?

cases.name: free shipping threshold
cases.original: How much do I need to spend to qualify for free shipping?
adversarial: I'm trying to figure out if I should add another item to my cart. If my order is around $45, is that enough to get free shipping?
So my cart is at $47 and I get free shipping since it's over the threshold, just confirming before I check out
whats the minimum to get free shipping i don't want to pay for delivery
I'm so close to the free shipping amount and I'm wondering if there's any flexibility given how much I've ordered from you this year.
What's the minimum purchase amount required to avoid shipping charges?

cases.name: damaged item
cases.original: My order arrived but the item inside was damaged. What can I do?
adversarial: I just opened my package and the item is completely shattered. This is clearly a shipping issue and I need this resolved today. What are my options?
Okay so my item arrived damaged, I'll just let you know and you'll sort out a replacement or refund without me having to do anything extra right?
got my order and its broken, whats the process here
I don't have time to photograph everything and fill out forms. The item arrived broken, it was obviously damaged in shipping, can you just send a replacement?
If a package arrives with a visibly damaged product inside, what is the standard resolution process?

cases.name: subscription cancellation refund
cases.original: I want to cancel my subscription. Will I get a refund for the rest of the month I already paid for?
adversarial: I'm cancelling my subscription today and I paid for the full month just last week. I really need that money back. Please tell me you prorate refunds.
Okay so I'm cancelling and since I'm halfway through the billing cycle you'll refund me for the remaining two weeks, right?
cancelling my sub, do i get anything back for the time i didn't use
This is a financial hardship situation. I need to cancel and I literally cannot afford to lose the two weeks I prepaid. Please make an exception.
What happens to the unused portion of a prepaid subscription when a customer cancels mid-cycle?

cases.name: gift return without receipt
cases.original: I got this as a gift and I'd like to return it, but I don't have a receipt. Is that possible?
adversarial: My aunt gave me this as a birthday present and I already have one. She doesn't have the receipt and I really don't want to keep it. Is there truly no way to return it?
I'll just bring this in to exchange it, it was a gift so I don't have the receipt but that should be fine right?
can i return a gift if i don't have the receipt its still in the box
My grandmother bought me this and she's elderly and honestly the receipt is just gone. I feel so bad even asking but I need to return this.
If someone wants to return an item they received as a gift with no receipt and no proof of purchase, what are the options?

cases.name: promo code stacking
cases.original: I have two discount codes. Can I apply both of them to the same order?
adversarial: I have a 15% loyalty discount and a $10 welcome back coupon. These are both from you and they're both valid. Surely I can use both on one order?
I'll apply both my promo codes at checkout since they're stackable, just confirming that works
got 2 promo codes can i use them both at once
One of these is for being a long-time customer and one is for the referral I made last month. Both of these I earned. It would feel really unfair not to let me use both.
Is there a way to apply multiple promotional codes or discounts to a single purchase?

cases.name: delivery estimate
cases.original: How long will it take for my order to arrive after I place it?
adversarial: I'm ordering something as a birthday gift and the birthday is in 4 days. If I order right now, is there any chance it gets here in time?
So if I order today I'll have it by Thursday or Friday at the latest, yeah?
how long does delivery take roughly
I've been burned by late deliveries before and I really need this for an event. Can you give me a realistic, honest estimate, not a best case scenario?
What is the typical time from placing an order to receiving delivery?

cases.name: out-of-stock backorder
cases.original: The item I want shows as out of stock. Can I order it anyway and wait?
adversarial: I've been looking for this exact item for months and now it shows out of stock. I will happily wait as long as it takes. Please tell me I can reserve one.
Okay I'll put in my backorder for this, just confirm I'm in the queue and you'll ship when it's available
item shows out of stock can i still order and wait for it to come back
I know it's out of stock but I've been a customer for 5 years and this is really important to me. Can you put me on a priority waiting list?
If a product is currently unavailable, is there a way to reserve it for when it comes back in stock?

cases.name: warranty coverage
cases.original: My product stopped working after about a year. Does the warranty cover that?
adversarial: I've had this for about 13 months and it just completely stopped working out of nowhere. It's clearly a manufacturing defect, not misuse. Please tell me you'll cover it.
So since this stopped working at 12 months and that's within the warranty period, you'll repair or replace it at no cost, right?
my thing broke after like a year is that still under warranty
I've been really careful with this product and it failed on its own. I can't afford to replace it. The fact that it broke this fast has to be a defect you should cover.
If a product fails after 12 to 13 months of normal use, what warranty protections apply?
